candidates.decision_reason: Strong source support and clear decision effect: the reader-facing page must point to the body track instead of replacing it.

candidates.candidate: Separate material body, AI explanation, audit evidence, and human confirmation into different product layers.
candidates.decision_reason: The separation directly shapes artifact layout and prevents internal audit from leaking into reader-facing prose.

candidates.candidate: Use the sequence preserve source, accompany reading, then capture candidates as a general product pattern.
candidates.decision_reason: Useful as an interpretive reading frame, but not yet proven as a reusable method across external material types.

candidates.candidate: Turn the product spec directly into a formal ReaderLab Skill.
candidates.decision_reason: The current demo only proves an internal longform body-track artifact, not a mature reusable Skill.

candidates.candidate: Treat fluent AI explanation as a substitute for reading the source.
candidates.decision_reason: Promoted only as a warning; this hazard determines the reader-facing reminder to start from the body file.
